thag15.2:1.2:
In AN 6:43, Ven. Udāyin recites these verses spontaneously in the
Buddha’s presence after the king’s elephant (nāga) has passed by,
thag15.2:1.3: and the Buddha defines the foremost nāga in these terms:
thag15.2:1.4:
“But, Udāyin, whoever in this world—with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās,
its people with their contemplatives & brahmans, their royalty & commonfolk—does no misdeed in body, speech, or mind:
thag15.2:2.1: That’s whom I call a nāga.”
thag15.2:2.2: The Buddha’s definition hints at a play on words:
thag15.2:2.3: “Does no misdeed,” in Pali, is āguṁ na karoti,
thag15.2:2.4: which could be rephrased as na āguṁ karoti, yielding a play on the word nāga.
thag15.2:3.1: In these verses, Ven. Udāyin shows that he has picked up on the hint by rephrasing it in precisely that way.
thag15.2:3.4: traveling along the Brahmā road, delighting in the stilling of the mind:
thag15.2:4.1: He, having gone beyond all dhammas,
thag15.2:4.2: to whom human beings pay homage,
thag15.2:4.3: the devas pay homage as well—
thag15.2:4.4: so I have heard from the Worthy One
thag15.2:5.2: gone from the forest to the clearing,
thag15.2:5.3: delighting in the renunciation of sensuality,
thag15.2:5.4:
released like gold from its ore.
He, the nāga outshining all others,
thag15.2:6.4: I will praise the nāga to you—
thag15.2:7.4: Austerity & celibacy are the nāga’s two other feet.
thag15.2:8.1: Conviction is the great nāga’s trunk,
thag15.2:9.4: Dhamma the balanced heat of his digestion;
thag15.2:10.2: He, in jhāna, delighting in assurance,
thag15.2:10.4: the nāga, when going, is concentrated,
thag15.2:11.1: when standing, the nāga is concentrated,
thag15.2:11.2: when reclining, the nāga is concentrated,
thag15.2:11.3:
when sitting, the nāga is concentrated.
Everywhere he’s restrained, the nāga:
thag15.2:13.3: wherever he goes, he goes without longing.
thag15.2:13.4: Like a white lotus, born & growing in the water,
thag15.2:14.1: but not smeared by the water
thag15.2:14.4: well-born in the world, lives in the world,
thag15.2:15.1: but is not smeared by the world,
thag15.2:15.2: like the lotus, by the water.
thag15.2:15.3: A great blazing fire unnourished grows calm,
thag15.2:16.3: this image is taught by the observant.
thag15.2:16.5: the nāga as taught by the nāga
thag15.2:17.1: free from aversion, free from delusion,
